questions.question_text: The presence of which of the following features is MOST helpful in distinguishing chronic from acute renal failure?

answers.text: Dilute urine with high urine sodium

questions.question_text: The two leading causes of end-stage renal disease are:
The leading genetic cause of end-stage renal disease is:
The leading cause of death among patients with end-stage renal disease is:
What stage of chronic kidney disease will have a patient with the glomerular filtration rate of 23 ml/min/1.73m2 according to the KDOQI classification?
Stage 4 and 5 of chronic kidney disease KDOQI may require the use of exogenous erythropoietin to manage:
Which class of drugs reduce heart rate, cardiac output, peripheral vascular resistance and renin secretion?
Uremia is described by which of the following statements:

answers.text: The concentration of urea in plasma.
The concentration of urea in the blood.
High levels of uric acid in the blood.
High levels or creatinine in the blood.
It is a syndrome which results from the significant loss of renal function.

questions.question_text: The leading cause of anemia in chronic kidney disease is:

answers.text: Insufficient levels of folic acid and vitamin B12

questions.question_text: When the decrease of glomerular filtration rate is considered physiological?

answers.text: After ingesting large quantities of liquids

questions.question_text: Which of the following clauses are included in the definition of chronic kidney disease?

answers.text: Kidney damage defined by structural or functional abnormalities of the kidney
The patient is undergoing a renal replacement therapy

questions.question_text: In which of the following situations you can put the diagnosis of end-stage renal disease (chronic kidney disease stage 5 KDOQI or End Stage Kidney Disease)?

answers.text: Patient is undergoing chronic peritoneal dialysis

questions.question_text: Which of the following are contraindications to renal transplantation?

answers.text: Serious conditions that are unlikely to improve after renal transplantation

questions.question_text: In a typical, uncomplicated pregnancy, which of the following renal changes occur?

answers.text: Proteinuria up to 500 mg/day and glycosuria

questions.question_text: Modifiable risk factors for progression of chronic kidney disease are:
Non-modifiable chronic kidney disease progression risk factors are:
Which methods are used to determine glomerular filtration rate in clinical practice?

answers.text: MDRD formula (Modification of Diet in Renal Disease)
CKD-EPI formula (Chronic Kidney Disease Epidemiology Collaboration)

questions.question_text: In the management of end-stage renal disease is included:
Which of the following describe chronic kidney disease:
Which of the following are used for the management of anemia in chronic kidney disease?
To slow the progression of chronic kidney disease, it is important to:

answers.text: Dihydropiridine calcium channel blockers (such as Amlodipine, Nifedipine)
Nondihydropiridine calcium channel blockers (such as Verapamil, Diltiazem)

questions.question_text: To which level it is recommended to reduce protein intake in a patient with chronic kidney disease?

answers.text: To 0.8 g/kg ideal weight per day + protein urinary loss
It is not recommended to reduce protein intake in patients with chronic kidney disease and severe malnutrition
To 1.5 g/kg ideal weight per day + protein urinary loss
To 2.0 g/kg ideal weight per day + protein urinary loss
To 3.0 g/kg ideal weight per day + protein urinary loss

questions.question_text: What drugs are used for the treatment of anemia in chronic kidney disease?
Which of the following are the principal recommendations to reduce the progression of chronic kidney disease?
Which of the following measures are strongly recommended for reducing the progression of chronic kidney disease?
Which of the following are the most frequent metabolic consequences of uremia?

answers.text: Impaired glucose tolerance and spontaneous hyperglycemia due to increased insulin resistance
The necessity of higher insulin doses

questions.question_text: Which of the following are the most frequent respiratory manifestations in chronic kidney disease?
Which of the following are the most frequent hematological manifestations in chronic kidney disease?

answers.text: Bleeding diathesis due to impaired platelets

questions.question_text: Which of the following are the most frequent bone and mineral manifestations in chronic kidney disease?

answers.text: Adynamic bone disease, due to abnormally low bone turnover
Kyphosis or lordosis, due to urea deposits in the intervertebral disk

questions.question_text: Which of the following are the most frequent neurological manifestations in chronic kidney disease?

answers.text: Ekbom syndrome, or the restless legs syndrome

questions.question_text: Which of the following are the most frequent gastrointestinal manifestations in chronic kidney disease?

answers.text: Uremic gastritis associated with anorexia, epigastric tenderness, vomiting and ammoniac smell
Peptic ulcer, due to increased gastrin levels in blood

questions.question_text: Which of the following lab results you will find in a patient with chronic kidney disease?
Which of the following electrolytic disorders you will most frequently find in a patient with chronic kidney disease?
Which of the following drugs are considered phosphate chelating agents (phosphate binders)?
Which of the following should be carried out before initializing chronic hemodialysis?

answers.text: Diet modification for a balanced intake of proteins, calories, vitamins and minerals

questions.question_text: Vascular access for hemodialysis can be achieved through:
Which of the following are criteria for definition of chronic kidney disease?

answers.text: Glomerular filtration rate <60 ml/min/1.73 m2 for ≥3 months, with or without kidney damage
Kidney damage for ≥3 months, as defined by structural or functional abnormalities of the kidney
The presence of markers of kidney damage, including abnormalities in the composition of blood or urine, or abnormalities in imaging tests
Glomerular filtration rate <90 ml/min/1.73 m2 for ≥3 months, with or without kidney damage
Congenital malformation without impaired renal function

questions.question_text: Which of the following groups of patients have an increased risk to develop chronic kidney disease?

answers.text: Patients with systemic diseases like systemic lupus erythematosus
Patients that have relatives with renal diseases

questions.question_text: Which of the following are the screening methods of patients with chronic kidney disease?

answers.text: Assessing the levels of urea and serum creatinine

questions.question_text: For a correct diagnosis of chronic kidney disease, it is necessary to:

answers.text: Determine the stage of the chronic kidney disease using KDOQI classification
Assess the levels of thyroid hormones

questions.question_text: Which of the following manifestations can be found more frequently in a patient with chronic kidney disease (CKD) stage 3 KDOQI?

answers.text: High blood pressure (in 50-60% of patients with CKD)
Increased levels of parathyroid hormone in the blood
Increased iron levels in the blood

questions.question_text: Which of the following manifestations can be found more frequently in a patient with chronic kidney disease (CKD) stage 5 KDOQI which are not treated with a renal replacement therapy?
Which are the objectives in the management of chronic kidney disease?

answers.text: Slowing the progressing of chronic kidney disease;
Preparing the patient with chronic kidney disease stage 3 KDOQI for a renal replacement therapy
Preparing the patient with chronic kidney disease stage 4 KDOQI for a renal replacement therapy
Enter the patient with chronic kidney disease stage 4 or 5 in the transplant waiting list

questions.question_text: Which are the typical hematological disorders in patients with chronic kidney disease
The treatment of hypervolemia in patients with chronic kidney disease is carried out by:

answers.text: The administration of diuretics in patients that do not undergo dialysis
The restriction of water and salt intake
The increase of water intake after dialysis procedures
The administration of diuretics and increase of water intake

questions.question_text: Which are the acute complications of hemodialysis

answers.text: It is the most frequent type of renal replacement therapy
It is performed 4-6 times per day, every day
It is more efficient than renal transplant
It is not performed in patients with post-surgical abdominal adhesions

questions.question_text: Which of the following sentences are true?

answers.text: In chronic kidney disease the kidneys size is usually normal
In acute kidney injury, you will always find size or structural modifications at a renal ultrasound exam
Anemia is frequent in chronic kidney disease
In acute kidney injury you can find severe renal bone disorders

questions.question_text: In which cases proteinuria can be considered normal?

answers.text: Oliguria with purpura and fever, associated with the syndrome of acute kidney injury (acute renal failure)

Proteinuria, hematuria, anemia and kidney failure

questions.question_text: Intrinsic acute renal failure is caused by:

answers.text: Structural damage of one of the segment of the nephron
Treatment with antihypertensive drugs (angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitors, sartans or calcium channel blockers)

questions.question_text: Which are the manifestations of acute kidney injure due to acute interstitial nephritis

answers.text: Edema, proteinuria, high blood pressure and acute renal failure
Unilateral lumbar pain, fever, pollakiuria, leucocyturia, hematuria and purpura
Fever, cutaneous rash, joint pains, eosinophilia, increased creatinine and sometimes the diuresis is normal
Epigastric tenderness, leukocytosis, constipations, stupor, increased creatinine

questions.question_text: Choose in which of the following disease, acute kidney injury can be manifested with intratubular obstructive deposits?

A type of chronic renal failure due to acute tubular necrosis
A type of prerenal acute kidney injury
Intoxication with a renal or hepatic toxin
Glomerulonephritis in patients with acute hepatitis

questions.question_text: Which is the main morphological lesion in ischemic or toxic acute kidney injury:

answers.text: Leukocytes infiltrations in the loops of Henle

questions.question_text: Hyperkalemia from acute kidney injury is treated with:

answers.text: Normalization of the levels of azotemia

answers.text: Renal artery stenosis of the solitary functional kidney

questions.question_text: Select the correct statements regarding acute tubular necrosis:

It is a common cause of acute kidney injury

answers.text: Acute kidney injury on chronic kidney disease

Excessive endogen production of water >400 ml/day
